{{Quote|Zayne has insisted on bringing us all into Mandalorian space aboard a comical-looking gunship crewed by creatures whose brains could not be found with a thorough autopsy.|[[Demagol]]|<ref name="Demagol journal">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/fiction/demagol/index.html?page=1|text="The Secret Journal of Doctor Demagol"|int=The Secret Journal of Doctor Demagol (article)|newhost=Suvudu}}</ref>}}
The '''''Moomo Williwaw''''' was a [[Pelagia Duplex Command Assault Gunship]] owned and operated by the [[Moomo Brothers]].

==Description==
[[File:MoomoWilliwaw-KOROTHB.png|thumb|left|200px|Cross-section of ''Moomo Williwaw'']]
The ''Moomo Williwaw'' was absurdly overarmed, featuring batteries of [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] and missiles in every conceivable arrangement. As noted above, several of the ''Williwaw''{{'s}} turreted weapons interfered with one another's firing arcs.

The Duplex Command Assault Gunship featured two separate cockpits, a design originally intended to allow the ship's co-captains to divide management of port and starboard armaments. The Moomo brothers, however, used these redundant piloting controls to wrest control of the vessel from one another—sometimes even during flight. The captains of passing starships have observed the ''Williwaw'' spin in place for as much as an hour while the Moomos settle their disputes.

==History==
The ''Williwaw'' was one of a line of [[Pelagia Duplex Command Assault Gunship]]s developed to defend the [[Tapani sector/Legends|Tapani sector]] during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Karoll Cilarnus]] admired the hardiness of the design and ordered the creation of a private model with double the standard armaments. Cilarnus' model was never officially christened, although workers in the drive yards nicknamed it "Overkill".

[[File:ZaynesGang.png|thumb|left|250px|The crew of the ''Moomo Williwaw''.]]
Baron Cilarnus' company was eventually placed in receivership and was unable to purchase the finished product. No conventional military would purchase the Overkill: it was poorly designed from a tactical perspective, with several weapons emplacements obstructing one another's arcs of fire.

The Overkill was set to be scrapped when it came into the possession of the [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] [[Del Moomo|Del]] and [[Dob Moomo]]. The Moomo brothers christened the vessel ''Moomo Williwaw'' and proceeded to add even more weapons to the ship's already extensive compliment—giving it "a few more sets of teeth", in Del's words.

In [[3963 BBY]], [[Marn Hierogryph]] hired the services of the Moomo brothers and the ''Moomo Williwaw'' to find Zayne, who was on [[Omonoth]]. They traveled to [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] where Gryph was helping the [[Taris Resistance|Resistance]]. Unfortunately, the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] sky patrol said that all captured vessels must go to the northern pole. Before doing so, Zayne, in [[Rohlan Dyre]]'s new [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Neo-Crusader]] armor, jumped down to the surface. While waiting for Zayne's call, Rohlan had [[Jarael]] spar with [[Darth Malak|Alek Squinquargesimus]].

[[File:Odryn_land.jpg|thumb|left|250px|''Moomo Williwaw'' lands on [[Odryn]].]]
Constable [[Noana Sowrs]] paid the Moomos to bring her children to [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], where in coincidence, the Mandalorians on [[Jebble]] were going to mount a full-scale invasion. The ''Williwaw'' retrieved Zyne and Gryph there, just as [[Cassus Fett]] bombarded the planet with [[Nuclear weapon/Legends|nuclear missiles]] to eradicate the [[Rakghoul plague/Legends|rakghoul plague]]. Acting on information of [[Celeste Morne]], the ''Williwaw'' went to [[Odryn]] to record images of the [[Sanctum of the Exalted]]. Jarael took the ''Williwaw'' to the edge of the system before returning to pick up Zayne and Gryph.

Armed with the evidence he needed to expose the [[Jedi Covenant]], the ''Williwaw'' headed to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Unfortunately, [[Lucien Draay]] claimed that Zayne was going to raid for the Mandalorians and the home fleet formed the [[Vanjervalis Chain]], preventing the ''Williwaw'' from getting close. So Zayne simply had them ram onto the [[Swiftsure (Inexpugnable-class)|''Swiftsure'']]{{'s}} hangar, where Zayne, Gryph and [[Slyssk]] stole the ''[[Deadweight]]''.

==Behind the scenes==
[[John Jackson Miller]] was inspired by the design of the [[Storm IV Twin-Pod cloud car/Legends|Twin Pod Cloud Car]].<ref name="JJM">{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor12.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #12''}}</ref> Artist [[Dustin Weaver]], who drew the cross-section of the ''Moomo Williawaw'', noted that it lacked a bathroom.<ref name="Handbook">{{Faraway|url=comics/swkotorhb1.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook #1''}}</ref>

In the [[Knights of the Old Republic 12|Reunion]] arc, [[Zayne Carrick]] sneaks aboard the ''Williwaw'' while [[Del Moomo]] is speaking to a hologram of [[Raana Tey]]. The screen behind Del appears to display a sideways view of Central America and Cuba.<ref name="K12">[[Knights of the Old Republic 12|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 12]]</ref>
